page_title: Where to Eat the Best Chicago-style Deep Dish Pizza in the World? | TasteAtlas
extraction_method: Playwright accessibility snapshot (WebFetch denied; TasteAtlas is a JS-rendered SPA)

places.name: Lou Malnati's Pizzeria
places.recommended_by: Steve Dolinsky and 11 other food critics
places.quote: Each bite has that perfect blend of cheese/crust/topping/sauce and that sauce – a rough mix of chunky tomatoes, sprinkled with grated pecorino romano seasoned with oregano – offers excellent acidity to balance the rich, melted cheese.

places.name: Pequod's Pizza
places.recommended_by: Steve Dolinsky and 9 other food critics
places.quote: Do I like the thick tomato sauce containing two types of California's finest? Sure. The slightly spicy, jagged-edged sausages made by a local butcher that are well-seasoned with oregano? Of course.

places.name: Pizano's Pizza
places.recommended_by: Jeff Ruby and 5 other food critics
places.quote: 10 great places to moon over a big pizza pie: Pizano's Pizza - The buttery crust tastes like pastry. The dough recipe, used for both the deep-dish and thin-crust pizza, is so carefully guarded that it's made by the owner's mother downstairs in the privacy of the basement.

places.name: Bartoli's Pizzeria
places.recommended_by: Steve Dolinsky and 5 other food critics
places.quote: This is his gift back to Chicago: an olive oil rich dough, where a gooey, melted, whole-milk mozzarella layer supports huge pieces of fennel and black pepper-kissed sausage, made by their family butcher; it's impressively juicy and meaty.

places.name: Pizzeria Uno
places.recommended_by: Michael Gebert and 5 other food critics
places.quote: Uno is where the original deep dish style -- a crispy short dough crust, topped with cheese first, then crushed tomatoes -- was invented in 1943 which later expanded to it's sister restaurant Pizzeria Due, a whole block away, when it caught on.

places.name: The Art of Pizza
places.recommended_by: Michael Gebert and 5 other food critics
places.quote: A Tribune poll some years ago named this the best deep dish in town, and as far as stuffed goes, we won't argue. It just gets everything right, from the flaky crust to the brightly spiced tomato sauce on top.

places.name: Louisa's Pizza & Pasta
places.recommended_by: Steve Dolinsky and 6 other food critics
places.quote: Baked for 25 minutes, the pizza manages to attain that perfect ratio of cheese to sauce to crust, which somehow maintains its exterior crispness.

places.name: Gino's East
places.recommended_by: Daniel Zemans and 3 other food critics
places.quote: The chunky, tangy sauce is a treat, even if it does have a bit too much oregano for my taste, and it goes well with the gooey pile of mozzarella.

places.name: Tano's Pizzeria
places.recommended_by: Steve Dolinsky and 1 other food critic
places.quote: On top of that base, a thin layer of mozzarella and some mildly spiced sausage, topped with an ultra-thin layer of dough and finally, the barest painting of tomato sauce. If you love bread, this is the pizza for you.

notes: WebFetch was denied for this URL, so a Playwright browser-based scrape was used. TasteAtlas is a JavaScript-rendered SPA. The page includes a 'LOAD MORE' button suggesting more entries exist beyond the initial 9, but the page auto-redirects to other pizza-category URLs (e.g. /pizza-napoletana/wheretoeat, /apizza/wheretoeat, /detroit-style-pizza/wheretoeat) within a few seconds of load — this happened repeatedly and prevented clicking LOAD MORE or running any awaited JS extraction. The 9 places listed here come from a clean accessibility-tree snapshot captured immediately after navigation. No numerical 1-5 rating/score is exposed on the list view; TasteAtlas instead shows a 'Recommended by [critic] and N other food critics' attribution, captured under 'recommended_by'. Detail URLs are constructed from the page-relative slugs found in the snapshot (e.g. /LouMalnatisPizzeria) prefixed with https://www.tasteatlas.com. To fetch entries beyond rank 9 or any 1-5 score, a more controlled scrape would be needed (e.g. server-side fetch with cookies, or a browser session that suppresses TasteAtlas's auto-rotation script before clicking LOAD MORE).
